Name: 5001 FLAVORS
Description:
Fashion has always had a large degree of relevance in the entertainment industry as actors, musical artists and sports stars often become just as famous for their sense of style as they are for their talent. A famous name is a valuable asset. Sports stars rule both on and off the field. And pop stars don’t just dominate the charts, they motivate markets. And increasingly, athletes and entertainers are turning themselves into “brands,” which puts even more pressure on all celebrities to do what they can to stand apart from the pack. Appearing at a premiere, on the red carpet, in a commercial or in a music video in an outfit that reflects their unique style and turns heads becomes of paramount importance.
Adhering to that need has been the success of 5001 FLAVORS.
5001 FLAVORS is a custom-made apparel company that has supplied the entertainment industry with a wide array of stylish designs and fashions since 1990. Since that time, such high-profile diverse clients as Will Smith, Aretha Franklin, Jennifer Lopez, Jamie Foxx, Lebron James, Queen Latifah, Nas and Shaquille O’Neal have commissioned the designers of 5001 FLAVORS to make butter-soft leather pants, high-octane fur jackets, sleek business suits and many other stylish garments. From Sean “P. Diddy” Combs to Mariah Carey, Scott Stapp to Timbaland, Hezekiah Walker to Usher, Jadakiss to Raven Symone, N’Sync to the late great Notorious BIG, 5001 FLAVORS maintains an impressive clientele list and formidable brand recognition in the music industry. The Harlem-based company’s clients have unique personalities, images and styles and look for fashion to reflect that.
The clothing produced by 5001 FLAVORS is as diverse as its clients. Because the goal of 5001 FLAVORS is to satisfy the needs of their clients’ lifestyles and tastes, the staff works tirelessly and enthusiastically to create new and innovative designs for all occasions, body types and professions. 5001 FLAVORS uses the latest fabrics and technology as needed for each project. The principals give their clients unwavering attention to ensure the outfits are to their ultimate satisfaction.
Now, the entertainment industry is resplendent with 5001 FLAVORS’ clothing, which can be seen in music videos, on album covers, in the movies, at the Grammy Awards, at the MTV Music Awards and in fashion editorials, as well as on stage and television. 5001 FLAVORS has established itself as the fundamental trendsetter for a diverse group of artists and record companies by supplying the entertainment industry with tailored clothing of the highest caliber. 5001 FLAVORS current client lists include sports figures, lawyers, music artists, producers, directors, television and film personalities as well as high-ranking record company executives. “I love their stuff,” says Fat Joe, who has been a client of the company’s for eight plus years.
5001 FLAVORS is the brainchild of talented Harlem-born designer, Guy Wood and his business partner Sharene Wood. The group launched the company after noticing a lack of unique style for many of the artists they had admired in the entertainment industry. Their instincts were right, as a flood of requests for 5001 FLAVORS fashions started pouring in, thanks to a word-of-mouth buzz. “It seemed as though one day, we had one client, and the next, 100,” says Sharene Wood. “We started in the early nineties and worked closely with some of that era’s hottest musical acts – Heavy D, Jodeci and emerging artist Mary J. Blige. One client lead to another and our business stared to flourish.”
5001 FLAVORS has since grown to include a fleet of experienced tailors, fashion stylists and a small administrative staff. The team works closely to complete each project in a timely fashion and to continuously build its client base while sustaining its relationships among its existing clients. Each year, 5001 FLAVORS gets many new clients as the entertainment industry continues to introduce new personalities and redefine old ones.
The process is simple. Usually, a client’s record label, assistant, manager or stylist will contact the company in search of an unique outfit. The client’s contact will then articulate to the 5001 FLAVORS staff the style and look the entertainer is looking for. The designers at 5001 FLAVORS come up with a design and the creation is brought to life. On average, it usually takes one week to turn around an outfit, but it also varies depending on the clients’ schedule and demand. In a week, the fitting takes place. Then the outfit is sent to appear in the next video, on stage, on the red carpet or wherever that celebrity client needs to be.
The success of 5001 FLAVORS, however, is centered on client relationships. The initial stage of the relationship is cultivated throughout the production process between 5001 FLAVORS and the client to ensure that the designs produced match the personalities and the vision of each client. A lasting relationship is built between the clients and the company as 5001 FLAVORS often maintains an artist as a client as they rise from obscurity to fame. The 5001 FLAVORS clothing is often the element of the artist’s appeal that sets them apart from others, making the design an indispensable component to the artist’s success. And artists indelibly become long-term and loyal customers.
Hard work and perseverance have enabled 5001 FLAVORS to rise from a virtually unknown company into a renowned manufacturer of the most stylish and innovative clothing available. The designing of new clothing, coupled with constant media exposure is translating into excellent potential growth opportunities for the company. Indeed, some of the hottest and most popular artists are wearing 5001 FLAVORS clothing and the demand is very high for its products and services. Its target market—the entertainment industry—is a never-ending source of business opportunity due to its constant need for 5001 FLAVORS products for its many personalities. But because of its exposure and rapid growth, 5001 FLAVORS’ consumer base has now expanded from beyond the exclusive entertainment industry and includes consumers from all walks of life. 5001 FLAVORS is now ready to expand its scope of business and broaden its horizons. “We’re exciting about the upcoming adventure, whatever it is,” ! says Guy Wood. So start to look for, recognize and enjoy the flavor of 5001 FLAVORS .
“Styles for the guy who wants to fit in.
Fashion for the lady who wants to stand out.
A brand that recognizes that you’re one of a kind.
5001 FLAVORS – a casual attitude and couture state-of-mind.”
“5001 FLAVORS is rooted in custom-made apparel.
Unique. One-of-a-kind. Carefully designed. Expertly tailored.
Unlike other lines of clothing that have been conceived to capitalize on celebrity, 5001 FLAVORS promotes its own design specialized heritage in each custom-made design.
As it has always done, 5001 FLAVORS emphasizes the individual.
5001 FLAVORS – It’s All About You”
“5001 FLAVORS is the fusion of popular entertainment culture,
Designer fashion and urban sensibilities.”
“5001 FLAVORS – For People With Great Taste”

Name: Beads of Paradise NYC
Description:
For over 34 years Beads of Paradise NYC has been dedicated to the art of designing and crafting handmade jewelry, in addition to collecting, showcasing and selling art, antiques and handicrafts from Africa, South East Asia and Mexico. Many of the crafts we buy and resell depend on hand finishing, repairing and refining existing handiwork, all of which we do in-house. We also work directly with clients to design and make custom one-of-a-kind jewelry made from gemstones and other materials that will become gifts and pieces of great sentimental value. Our great love is to cross a diverse array of cultural interests by bringing beads, objects, raw materials, and antique items to New York City, so our clients can explore to the possibilities of connecting with other cultures and history in a significant and relatable way. We have made presentations at the Rubin Museum and the Metropolitan Museum on beadwork and sourcing Culturally Significant Arts, Crafts and Jewelry. In our workplace in the easy to access Union Square / Flatiron district, we hold classes weekly as outreach to people wanting to understand jewelry making. These classes include Elders as well as Students of all different ages and circumstances. Classes have also been given in Local Schools to encourage creativity and openness to other cultures.
We have a wealth of clients that began coming to Beads of Paradise as children with their parents, and have continued to come as they grow into teens, as they become adults and parents themselves, and with their own children and grandparents they start a new generation of the Beads of Paradise family. We serve designers and artists, costume departments and museums, casual beaders to professional jewelry makers, to those just looking to see and experience something new, beautiful and interesting. We are here for the Community — a colorful, friendly, inclusive little world of its own inside New York City.

Name: Blue Tin Production
Description:
Founded by Hoda Katebi and lauded by Business of Fashion and Vogue, Blue Tin Production Co-Op is an apparel manufacturing workers co-operative run by immigrant, refugee, and working-class women of color in Chicago, Illinois. We cut and sew in house, do not require minimum order quantities, deliver production sustainability specs, and each member of our team boasts over ten years of industrial sewing experience across categories to satisfy luxury quality standards. Blue Tin is not only meeting global standards in sustainability, but also actively reimagining and delivering upon an in-house multi-step process to reduce waste that includes implementing unusable textiles as packing materials and for community sewing classes, and composting as part of local circular economies.
As a worker-owned and run co-operative, all of the members receive healthcare, mental health support, access to social services, paid professional development, on-site interpreters, childcare, educational and wellness training, and a community devoted to supporting one another. Every member of our team has been and will continue to be women of color, trans, gender non-conforming, intergenerational, queer, working-class folx, and/or immigrants and refugees. Our work is centered on redesigning fashion industry supply chains and crafting a model for structural change at the manufacturing level.

Name: E&J Handbags

Company Info.Address: 33 E 33rd Street, Unit #803 New York , NY , 10012

Description:
Our factory employs eight craftsmen and women. We have over 20 specialized machines from sewing machines, clicker for cutting, spraying machine for glue, 360 crease machine for stitching, heavy stitch machines, and optitex technology. The factory is in a light filled studio off of park avenue with seemingly endless rolls go beautiful fabrics and exotic leathers covering the walls. Our team of craftsmen is led by Farid Gadalla who comes from a long family history of makers and artisans. Farid possesses a unique ability to transform any idea into a detailed, multi-piece pattern both by hand and digitally with Optitex. You can loosely describe an idea or sketch to Farid and he can produce it immediately, from the pattern to the final product. Farid took over his father’s Egypt-based leather goods factory when Farid was 21 years old. Once he had children of his own, he moved his family to the U.S. and taught himself english while working random jobs. He began working in the studio a small handbag maker where he caught the eye of COACH New York. During the 15 years he was at COACH, he created his own patent, worked as a pattern maker, manager, senior manager, quality controller, and trainer for Coach’s factories in Vietnam and India. After leaving COACH, Farid continued to work in Asia for two more years with TBS Group based in Vietnam as the director and quality manager for the Handbag Division.
Today he is the very loved leader and teacher of our team. Farid is eager to make any product imaginable. He offers design, pattern making, and production. He truly does it all, like magic! Farid’s current customers include Kaila Katherine, and others (NDAs).

We work with brands large and small, but we have a very soft spot for fashion start-ups who seek direct support to help build a strong surface design identity.
Our focus is creating emotionally resonant patterns that align with each client’s brand identity, seasonal design directions, future consumer sentiments, and macro trends within the target audience.
We offer a comprehensive suite of services tailored to fashion designers, including exclusive original artworks, bespoken custom designs, design indexing, color variations, production print pack preparation, yarn dye design and tech pack, vintage design restoration, as well as seasonal color and print trend forecasting supported by AI-generated digital assets and inclusive and diverse AI model visualization.
At HOUSE OF JUNE™, we take the heavy load of producing original print designs off our client’s shoulders so they can focus on their overall creative visions.
Our mission is providing fashion brands with unique artworks that inspire their creative teams while driving business growth. We achieve this through cultivating long-term partnerships, ensuring our clients receive the specialized surface design support they need throughout the development process – from concept to production.

Name: Industrial Sewing and Innovation Center (ISAIC)
Description:
ISAIC – Industrial Sewing and Innovation Center
More than a sewing and innovation center, ISAIC is a national resource for those committed to positive impact through responsible production of high-quality garments with solutions centered around people, education, talent force development and cutting-edge technology.
Our facility is not just a factory, it’s a philosophy.
Located above Carhartt’s flagship retail store in Midtown Detroit, in a space donated and built out by the clothing giant, the ISAIC factory is equipped to meet the needs of today’s brands. Capable of handling large contracts for government and national brands, we can also accommodate short runs with modular cells for on-demand orders.
We believe that sustainability must start with people. When you manufacture with ISAIC, your business invests in our nonprofit institute, which develops an equitable, local workforce, trained in traditional skills and the latest technologies. We are committed to better best practices and making an authentic difference in ethical manufacturing.
Together, with like-minded companies, we are revolutionizing the industry.
ISAIC has highly skilled sewing technicians at the heart of our services. As a center for testing innovation, automation and technology, we’re constantly adding to our portfolio. Current resources include: programmable (CNC) cutting, programmable and multiple stitch sewing, circular knitting, embroidery, digital printing and so much more.
For emerging designers and startups, we have a network of partners to refer those with needs for sourcing fabrics, tech packs and patterns.
Learn more at www.isaic.org.

Our studio helps designers realize their concepts with expert craftsmanship, fast turnaround, and the accountability that comes with local, in-person production. Our goal is to deliver garments of the highest quality with exceptional efficiency.
For more information, please visit our website at www.oneto13studio.com or follow us on Instagram @oneto13studio.

Company Info.Address: 265 w 37th st 4th fl New York , NY , 10018

Description:
At One Stop, we know how much work goes into being a success in fashion.
Having been in at the heart of the garment district for over 30 years, we have not only been involved in launching and developing brands but we have also built honest relationships with the local businesses around us.
We have been helping apparel design companies with pattern making and clothing manufacturing, to their great satisfaction. We understand how overwhelming it can be, and we also know that it is almost impossible to do it alone. Outside of the listed services, we also offer digitizing.
Putting together the ease of developing a product, we formulate the creativity and inspiration that you bring into reality. We become the partner, team, and powerhouse you need to make a statement in this industry.
Everyone that walks through our doors, speak of the comfort that our facilities bring.
Our clients become like family. Our facilities are one of the cleanest places you will ever find in New York City.
We believe that our staff use their God given talents, from pattern-makers, cutters, sewers, pressers, to finishers to serve you with the best of our ability!

Name: OnPoint Manufacturing
Description:
OnPoint Manufacturing is an eco-friendly, on-demand apparel manufacturer based in Florence, AL. We provide our clients with a better and more profitable way to produce and sell their garments. We are unique in the apparel manufacturing business. We require no minimum order runs since every garment is manufactured one at a time. Minimum orders can be as low as one. This model virtually eliminates manufacturing waste and excess inventory for our clients.
Using advanced technology, we produce and ship garments within three to five days of receiving the order. We offer complete fulfillment services, so your garment can be shipped to its final destination in your special packaging.
OnPoint works with a variety of designers and brands including the sustainable womenswear brand Katla and traditional Austrian loden jacket designer Robert W. Stolz.

Description:
With over 50 years’ experience as a cut-and-sew manufacturing facility in York, Pennsylvania, veteran owned and led Perform Group LLC is committed to providing its customers with superior products, unparalleled customer service and uncompromising
quality with the seal of ‘Made in USA’ at its heart. We continually demonstrate our ‘on time, every time’ promise to both new and long standing accounts.
By offering integrated design services for custom textile and apparel requests, we are proud to accommodate rapid new product releases in both small and large batch ordering. Our automated handling unit production system (UPS) lines drive efficiency and speed up production. With quality inspection processes, orders are fulfilled not only
quickly but accurately and to our high standard of quality. Lean manufacturing methodology ensures we minimize waste while maximizing productivity. Our clients benefit from our established long term raw material partners, which means greater supply options at lower price points without sacrificing quality.
Specializing in high performance fabrics, active and athletic wear, Perform Group has partnered with a variety of companies to produce a diverse range of cut-and-sew items. Most importantly, we take great pride in every stitch and aim for full satisfaction from both our clients and the end wearers.
We offer:
-Design Services
-Product development
-Sample making
-Pattern making
-Cut-and-sew manufacturing
-Supply chain management
-Raw material sourcing
-Inspection
-Warehousing
-Order fulfillment
// Our equipment includes commercial sewing machines, Gerber cutter, heat press, embroidery and embellishment placement machinery.

Aside from our employees we also utilize two fully automated Gerber Cutters to ensure precise, efficient cutting.
We believe that nothing can compare to a NYC manufacturing experience due to the quality of the service and convenience of having the services done locally. We have worked with a wide range of clients who are part of emerging brands to well-known globally recognized brands who specialize from athletic wear to casual wear up until formal dress wear.

We are a Garment District based MWBE* boasting a combined 20+years in the fashion industry. Never one to say no to a designer’s vision, we provide the icing on top by developing custom trimmings, branding materials, and accessories to help designers large and small create their unique voice.
We have been a part of many incredibly rewarding projects guided by designers from all parts of the industry. With clients like Pyer Moss, Dreamville, Lily Pulitzer, Adam Selman, and Coach every day at the office is another chance to put wearable art into the world.

Name: Tuscarora Mills
Description:
Tuscarora Mills is an independent Pennsylvania based natural fiber and fabric manufacturer of apparel, home goods and decorative fabrics.
We weave 33′, 56′ and wide (90 – 120′) widths on Rapier dobby, Draper denim and jacquard looms.
We source yarns from American and European mills in a wide range of dry and wet spun OE and RS construction and counts.
Competitive prices for dye, scour, and finishing services are available through our supply chain partners.
Our goals are straightforward:
-Weave fabric in America and work to renew the American textile supply chain, with fabrics of quality, style, beauty and value.
-Reduce our dependence on oil as a base material for plastic and man-made fibers.
-Work with nature, not against, by using natural hemp, flax, organic cotton and wool fiber, which is regenerative and biodegradable.
-Contribute to local and regional economies, which nurture communities and honor labor the art and craft of textile, apparel and industrial design and manufacturing.
-Curtail our dependence on global supply chains fueled by oil and greed effectively stealing
capital and jobs from the United States and Americans.
This is our mission.
The support of designers, brands, industry and consumers like yourself creating demand for natural fiber fabrics is vital to the renewal of America’s textile tradition, our communities and fighting the causes and effects of our global climate crisis.

Name: TWO ONE TWO NEW YORK, INC.
Description:
Two One Two NY, Inc. is one of the largest, fully vertical domestic textile manufacturers on the East Coast.  We are women owned and family operated in New York since our inception in 1998.
Our extensive product assortment covers all textile categories including sweater knits, knit dressing and accessories as well as soft goods for the home and hospitality industries and cut and sew (knits and wovens).
We serve a broad customer base of major retailers, chains, department stores and e-com entities.
As a 100% domestic / NY manufacturer, our niche is the ability to provide fast fashion at competitive pricing.  With a shortened production and delivery cycle we have the ability to provide rapid replenishment thereby keeping our customers’ style relevant with minimum inventory exposure.
Our business model focuses on product development and with all functions from design to delivery under one roof, we are able to work directly with our customers’ product development and sourcing teams to deliver quality, at competitive prices and at a fraction of the time as compared to offshore production.

Name: Western Carolina Sewing Company
Description:
Sew Co. is a living-wage relational sewing factory in Asheville, NC.  We facilitate textile-based projects at all stages – from prototyping and patternmaking to small-batch and large scale production.   We have been in business for 13 years, and are a diverse team with backgrounds in fashion design, pattern making, costuming, and fine art.
Our company culture is one of transparency and empowered employee voice.  We play the Great Game of Business which is radical financial literacy and transparency with each employee, every week.   We are a zero waste facility in that we recycle all our textile waste with a local company that is processing it into a new yarn to use for knitting and weaving new textiles – so a super local, circular economy here in WNC!  We have helped build industrial sewer training programs with in our local community colleges and have established the first textile pattern-making apprenticeship in NC.
Our portfolio of clients include:
Quiet Town Home,
East Fork Pottery,
Elizabeth Suzann Studio,
Adelene Simple Cloth, &
Natalie Koromoto
…among many others.
We prioritize relationships with partners who find value alignment in our work together.  Our manufacturing practices honor the cloth, the people, and the environment.

Name: Wing & Weft Gloves
Description:
Wing & Weft Gloves is the only glove factory in New York City’s Garment Center. Our mission is to preserve and nurture craftsmanship in the United States. We are a small, women-owned business and we strongly support other small businesses, start-ups, new designers, and students. We want to make your glove-related dreams come true!
We are proud to have produced gloves for the Theatre, Film, Drag, and Fashion communities for over 40 years. Every glove you purchase sustains our legacy.
We work on any scale. Wing & Weft has made a single pair of gloves for a student putting together their first collection, over two dozen pairs in less than a week for a runway show, hundreds of identical pairs for a medical technology company… if you are looking for someone to sew gloves for you for any reason, we are your factory!
Please feel free to get in touch whether you are looking for a pair of gloves for yourself, interested in producing a run, or just want a tour of our space.
We glove you!

While we are primarily an OEM/ODM Jewelry Manufacturer collaborating with esteemed Maisons, we welcome private clients and starting jewelry designers alike. Contact us today to learn more about our services, including: Advanced CAD Development, 3D Stone Scanning, Bespoke Mountings, Custom Eternity Bands, Exquisite Stone Setting, High-Quality Polishing, Rhodium Gold Plating, 3D Printing & Prototyping, Precious Metals Casting, CNC Machining, Vitreous Enameling, Laser Engraving, Jewelry Repairs, Antique Restoration, Custom Private Jewelry, Appraisals.
